ref: Laban named the pile of rocks Jegar Sahadutha. But Jacob named it Galeed.

translated: Leah also named her son Judah, and Jacob also named his son Gad.

ref: Jacob knew it was Joseph's coat and said, “It's my son's coat! Joseph has been torn to pieces and eaten by some wild animal.”
text: O nyaasi u, ka baŋŋi u, ka sagi, “Mm, m bii maa tagite n na, ama moo puu buŋ n gbaag u toog u ŋobi.”

ref: Leah shouted, “God has rewarded me for letting Jacob marry my servant,” and she named the boy Issachar.
text: Liya yeli, “Naawunni yo ma samini, a kpali ni naa m bipoga ti m sira zu.” Ka digili bii maa yuuri Isaka.
translated: And she said, “Who would have told Abraham that Sarah would nurse children? Yet I have borne him a son in his old age.” And the child was named Isaac.

ref: When he got there, he let the camels rest near the well outside the city. It was late afternoon, the time when the women came out for water.
text: O n wa ti paa tiŋŋa maa, o ti nyogimasi maa gba tiŋŋa, bulugu see, zaanoori jemani, jemanso pogiba ni wara kulisi.
translated: He was a very handsome man, and his wife was also beautiful, with fair skin, long hair, and very white teeth.

ref: God told Jacob, “Return to Bethel, where I appeared to you when you were running from your brother Esau. Make your home there and build an altar for me.”
text: Niŋi n na, Naawunni yeli Yakubu “Nyisigi n gaa Betel wa ti zi, ka me sariga daaŋu ti mani Naawunni, so n daa n ta e saani, ka e daa n kpali e mabii Isawu zu zora la”.
translated: Now, God said to Jacob, "Arise and go up to Bethel and dwell there; and make an altar there to God, who appeared to you when you fled from the face of your brother Esau."

ref: Jacob got up in the middle of the night and took his wives, his eleven children, and everything he owned across to the other side of the Jabbok River for safety.

text: Niŋi n na, Sekem, Hamo so n niŋi Hivati nira, ka niŋi paaligu maa naaba biduwa, nye Dayina, ka gbaag u wa ti bibi u kpabi.

ref: Isaac took Rebekah into the tent where his mother had lived before she died, and Rebekah became his wife. He loved her and was comforted over the loss of his mother.
text: Yisaaka mari Rabeeka kpe o ma gbana sugiri puwa, a naag u loobi o poga, ka daa n kpabira u ga. O ma kum sufusaaŋu n daa mari u, sufupeelim pa ta.
translated: Isaac loved Rebecca very much, but she was barren, and he prayed to God for her. God heard his prayer, and Rebecca became pregnant.
